channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: The Wolf Project - Reworking a Real-World Project
item.description: A few weeks ago I watched a Dodo video about The Wolf Project that stayed with me more than I expected. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NMf8mlxO4sk After that, I tried to find more information...

item.title: Aximo - offline-first STT API
item.description: Finally got Aximo running publicly on Hugging Face Spaces — local CPU speech-to-text API with Swagger microphone recording, powered by Parakeet v3. Demo: https://ifif-aximo.hf.space/docs Repo:...

item.title: code-shot: the code-screenshot API that I wish existed
item.description: For seven years Carbon has been the canonical "make a code screenshot" tool. Same with Ray.so since 2020. Both are fantastic — and both are design surfaces, not endpoints. If I'm writing a dev...

item.title: The Hidden Cost of AI Programming (and How to Use It Mindfully)
item.description: “AI didn’t break your code. You just trusted it too much.” AI tools like GitHub Copilot and ChatGPT are changing how we write software. You type a comment… and suddenly a full function appears....

item.title: Beyond the Next '26 Keynote: How to Actually Build a Secure Agent with Remote MCPs (step-by-step)
item.description: This is a submission for the Google Cloud NEXT Writing Challenge The recent Google NEXT `26 developers keynote was full of really groundbreaking and exciting stuff, but there was one particular...

item.title: Building a real-time multiplayer hub on Nakama in 4 weeks: socket events to Zustand, what I'd do differently
item.description: Quick context I'm a solo dev building SQL Protocol, a browser game where you play a covert operative and every mission is a real SQL query against a real Postgres database. Three modes: 15-chapter...

item.title: @agent — Code Annotations for AI Agents
item.description: @agent — Code Annotations for AI Agents AI agents are now a routine part of how code gets read, refactored, and extended. They are a fourth audience alongside humans, compilers, and IDEs — but...

item.title: Canonical lays out a plan for AI in Ubuntu Linux
item.description: One of the most popular Linux distributions is about to get an influx of AI features. As reported by Phoronix, Jon Seager, VP of engineering at Ubuntu developer Canonical, shared a blog post on...

item.title: Chapter 8: RMS Normalisation and Residual Connections
item.description: What You'll Build Two architectural patterns that make deep networks trainable: RMSNorm (keeps activations from exploding or vanishing) and residual connections (gives gradients a highway to flow...

item.title: I pointed the OpenAI SDK at my friend's gaming PC. It just worked.
item.description: I changed two strings in a Python script — base_url and api_key — and it stopped calling OpenAI. Instead, the request travelled across the public internet, into a Podman container running on a...

item.title: Three men are facing 44 charges in Toronto SMS Blaster Arrests
item.description: Article URL: https://www.tps.ca/media-centre/stories/unprecedented-sms-blaster-arrests/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47927070 Points: 4 # Comments: 0

item.title: Elon Musk Boosts New Yorker’s Sam Altman Exposé on X as Trial Begins
item.description: The move comes as the trial for Elon Musk’s lawsuit against OpenAI kicks off in federal court in Oakland.

item.title: Trump demands ABC fire Jimmy Kimmel
item.description: President Donald Trump is calling for Disney to fire Jimmy Kimmel. On Thursday, Kimmel joked that Melania Trump had looked like an "expectant widow" in a skit about the upcoming White House...

item.title: Google employees ask Sundar Pichai to say no to classified military AI use
item.description: Over 600 Google employees signed a letter to CEO Sundar Pichai demanding that Google block the Pentagon from using its AI models for classified purposes, reports the The Washington Post. Its...

item.title: OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal
item.description: OpenAI has won major concessions from its largest shareholder, Microsoft, that will allow it to sell products on AWS, while Microsoft gets more cash in a revenue-share agreement.

item.title: DeepMind’s David Silver just raised $1.1B to build an AI that learns without human data
item.description: Ineffable Intelligence, a British AI lab founded a mere few months ago by former DeepMind researcher David Silver, has raised $1.1 billion in funding at a valuation of $5.1 billion.

item.title: Conspiracy theories are swirling about the White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting
item.description: On Saturday evening, a room full of journalists, media personalities, and senior members of Donald Trump's administration descended into chaos as gunshots rang out at the White House...

item.title: Why the Steam Controller is (and isn’t) a big deal
item.description: Most PC gamers already have a controller they love using with Steam - a Sony DualSense, a 8BitDo Ultimate, a Nintendo Switch Pro, or something else. Part of that love comes from Steam treating...

item.title: Valve’s new Steam Controller isn’t perfect, but I’m buying one anyway
item.description: After my brief hands-on last year with Valve's new Steam Controller, I said it might be my dream controller. I've been looking for a controller with the customization and sheer function available...

item.title: Valve launches the Steam Controller without the Steam Machine
item.description: Last November, Valve introduced the world to its new vision of living room gaming: the Steam Machine and Steam Controller. Then, RAMageddon. Memory shortages forced Valve to delay all its hardware...

item.title: We reviewed Valve’s new Steam Controller, ask us anything
item.description: Hey hey, it's Jay Peters, senior reporter at The Verge. Today, Valve finally announced that the second version of the Steam Controller - and the first piece of Valve's slate of new gaming hardware...

item.title: Samsung’s first smart glasses have leaked
item.description: The upcoming Samsung Galaxy Glasses look nearly identical to Meta's Ray-Ban smart glasses in leaked images from Android Headlines. The glasses, which could be announced at Google I/O next month,...

item.title: Microsoft and OpenAI’s famed AGI agreement is dead
item.description: OpenAI and Microsoft's partnership-turned-situationship just got even less committed. And a clause about artificial general intelligence, which has for years dictated the future of their deal, has...

item.title: The missing step between hype and profit
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. In February, I picked up a flyer at an anti-AI march in...

item.title: Investors back Skye’s AI home screen app for iPhone ahead of launch
item.description: Skye's new AI app attracted investors before it even launched — a sign of interest in a more AI-aware iPhone.

item.title: Checkmarx Confirms GitHub Repository Data Posted on Dark Web After March 23 Attack
item.description: Checkmarx has disclosed that its ongoing investigation tied to the supply chain security incident has revealed that a cybercriminal group published data related to the company on the dark web....

item.title: The Man Behind AlphaGo Thinks AI Is Taking the Wrong Path
item.description: David Silver has a new billion-dollar company that aims to build AI “superlearners.”

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Fast16 Malware, XChat Launch, Federal Backdoor, AI Employee Tracking & More
item.description: Everything is dumb again. This week feels broken in a very familiar way. Old tricks are back. New tools are doing shady crap. Supply chains got hit. Fake help desks worked. Weird research showed...

item.title: China blocks Meta’s $2B Manus deal after months-long probe
item.description: China has ordered Meta to unwind its multibillion-dollar Manus acquisition, dealing a potential setback to Zuckerberg’s push into AI agents.

item.title: OpenAI could be making a phone with AI agents replacing apps
item.description: There have been plenty of rumors about OpenAI's hardware plans, which involve launching a pair of earbuds. A new note from industry analyst Ming-Chi Kuo suggests that the AI company might be...
